user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows the rear bumper area with visible damage, satisfying [REQ_CAR_BODY_PANEL] and [REQ_GENERAL_OBJECT_PART].
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 directly shows the dented rear bumper area claimed by the customer.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows front bumper damage matching the claimed scratch; img_2 provides a full front view but no damage, still meeting the requirement as img_1 satisfies the evidence need.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows clear scratch/damage on the front bumper, directly supporting the claim; img_2 shows the front of the car without damage but does not contradict the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the claimed car windshield with an extensive crack/fracture pattern, satisfying the glass crack inspection requirement; img_2 is usable but not needed to establish the claim.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a car windshield with a large spiderweb crack/fracture pattern matching the claimed stone-impact windshield crack. img_2 shows a windshield area but no visible crack, so it is insufficient rather than supportive.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the claimed car side mirror with visible cracking of the mirror glass, satisfying the mirror component inspection requirement.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows the claimed red car side mirror with spiderweb cracking in the mirror glass, supporting visible damage to the claimed part; misalignment is not clearly demonstrated but mirror damage is visible.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Rear bumper visible in img_2, no damage observed; img_1 shows front fender dent, unrelated to claim.
claim_status_justification: Rear bumper visible in img_2, no damage; img_1 shows front fender dent, unrelated to claim.
